title: Active team state and canonical queue authority are separate project facts

sources.observation: continuity_mining is active and tasked with recovering project history and updating the knowledge substrate
M-001 is the only continuity_mining-owned item and is completed
CM-0010 records the absence of an active owned queue item without self-authorizing M-002
the base index ends at CM-0009 and therefore does not yet link the later queue-ownership-gap record

observed: The repository can simultaneously define a team as active and contain no active queue item owned by that team.
inferred: Team lifecycle state describes organizational responsibility, while queue state grants bounded execution authority; neither should be substituted for the other.
proposed: Future continuity tooling should model team_status and execution_authority as separate nodes joined by dated queue-assignment edges.
superseded: The implicit assumption that active team status alone authorizes selecting new canonical work is superseded for this repository state.
unresolved: No canonical owner or rule currently specifies who may add M-002 after the prior owned item is completed.
falsification_or_review_route: Re-read all four cited blobs. Falsify the observed claim if ACTIVE_QUEUE.json at the cited SHA contains an active or queued continuity_mining item, or if CURRENT_STATE.json does not mark the team active. Resolve the unresolved claim only with a later canonical rule or queue assignment that identifies the authorizing owner.
implementation_boundary: This additive graph extension does not modify ACTIVE_QUEUE.json, reopen M-001, create M-002, or claim peer execution.
